raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/XXXX  I purchased 4 guitar cases from XXXX on XXXX for {$230.00}. The vendor stated in their listing that 100 % customer satisfaction was guaranteed and that return shipping would be paid by the vendor. ( I have screen shots proving this and I provided them to CITI CARDS. ) When I asked to return the items, XXXX sent me to a webpage that provided me with four postage-paid return labels THAT WERE ALL EXACTLY THE SAME. I packed up the items, attached the labels, and brought them to the Post Office, where I was told that none of the labels had enough postage to return even one of the items. XXXX subsequently told me that the cost to return them was more than they were worth and offered to refund me {$5.00}. I could not find a way to contact XXXX, which I'm sure was their intention. So I contacted CITI CARDS ( in XXXX  ) which promises to protect its customers from fraud such as this. CITI refused my dispute. They have given various excuses for this at various times. They claimed I didn't prove that the vendor did not honor their promises, which is false. They also claimed that they could not refund my money because I didn't return the items! This is clearly a Catch 22 and it is THEIR fraudulent rule, which they could change. They have also claimed, recently, that there is nothing they can do because it has been so long. Lie. CITI is in cahoots with criminals on XXXX.
